label: Recursive partitioning & ensembling

node.label: Recursive partitioning & ensembling
node.definition: Decision regions are recursively divided and multiple predictors are combined to reduce variance or bias.
node.why_it_matters: The role of Recursive partitioning & ensembling is to connect Algorithms & data structures, Statistics & experimental design to downstream capabilities including Decision trees, random forests & boosted trees, Self-consistency & best-of-N inference.
examples: substrate for Decision trees, random forests & boosted trees
limitations: Its benefit depends on implementation, scale, data, and hardware; the same mechanism can add complexity without improving the target workload. Its direct dependencies include Algorithms & data structures, Statistics & experimental design.

dependencies.rationale: Recursive partitioning & ensembling uses Algorithms & data structures as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
Recursive partitioning & ensembling uses Statistics & experimental design as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
Recursive partitioning & ensembling uses Supervised learning as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
